California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May ■6′  ■99■
Peter C. Carton
School LegaI Services
P.0. Box 
■■1■0
Bakersfield, CA
93389
Re:  Your Request for lnformal AdviCe
Our File No. I-90-■
66
Dear Mr. carton:
You have requested advice on behalf of the san Luis coastal
Unified school District concerning appl-icatj'on of the confl'ict-of-
interest provisions of the PoIiti;al- Reform Act (the rrg6lrr;1 65
they pertaj.n to board member Caroline Botwin. Thj.s letter
summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you on March 2L,
1990 and again on Apri.l .1 , 1991 .
I also advised you that our advice is lini.ted only to the
provisions of the Act. we cannot provide advice about other
restrictions or con f I ict-of - interest laws, such as Government Code
section 109o or the doctrine of incornpatible activities. 
PIease
contact the Attorney General''s office for advice regarding these
areas of law.
OUEST I ON
Under the Act, may board nember Botwin participate in schoo]
district 
decisions regarding compensation, approval of salary war-
rants and collective bargaining, since she is also a teacher in
the distr ict?
1- Government code Sections 8l-o0o-91015. All statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indi-cated. Commission
reguLations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seo. AlL references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J StrcCt,Suite 800 0 P.0.Box 307 0 Sacramento CA 95804‐0807 ● (916)322‐ 5660


our File No. I-90-1.56
Page 2
CONCLUSION
Ms. Botwin nay participate in decisions regarding an increase
or decrease of salaries of aII teachers in the same classification
as herself. 
she may also participate in coLLective bargaining
decisions concerning her bargaining unit as long as these
decisj.ons affect herself and aII employees in the same job
classification in the same manner.
IAEIS
Ms. Botwin is both a trustee of the district 
and a teacher
and paid enployee of the district. 
As a trustee, Ms. Botwin will
be plrticiplting 
in numerous decisions, sone of which include:
cornpensation for classified staff, approval of the nonthly
certificated salary $/arrants (including her oi.rn ) , and changes to
the colLective bargaining agreements for certificated 
and
classified staff.
ANALYSIS
The Act prohibits a publj"c official 
frorn making,
-rri::cipating in, or using her official position to influence a
governmlnta 1 
- dec is ion j'n wnich she knows or has reason to know she
6as a financial interest. 
(Section B7rOO.) A public official 
has
a financial interest in a decisj.on if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision wiIl have a material financial effect,
distinguishable fron its effect on the public generalJ'y, on the
officiil 
or a member of her i:nnediate family or on, among other
things:
(a) Any business entity in which the pubLic
official 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars (S1000) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars (S10o0) or more.
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a conmercial Iending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating tvo hundred fifty
dollars (5250) or more in value provided to,
received by or pronised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the tine when the
decision is nade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of rnanagement.


Our File No. I-90-■66
Page 3
(e) Any donor of, or any interrnediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
tuo hundred fifty dollars (5250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official lrithin 12 nonths prior to the ti:ne when
the decision is nade.
Section 87103 (a) - (e) .
As a public official, 
Ms. Botvin nay not participate in a
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a naterial financiaL effect on any of her econornic interests.
The Act focuses only on financial interests that nay become
disqualifying interests.
Section 82030(b) (2) excludes fron the definition of income
any salary and reinbursement for expenses or per dien received
from a state, locaI or federal government agency. Therefore,
!1s. Botwin's salary which she receives as a teacher for the
district 
does not constitute a disqualifying interest pursuant to
Section 87103 (c) .
Regulation la702- 1(a) (4) (copy enclosed) prohibits a public
official 
fron participating in any decision which would
foreseeably increase or decrease the personal expenses, income,
assets or liabilities 
of the official 
or her inmediate farnily by
at least 5250. However, that regulation also provides:
(c) Notwithstanding subsection (a) an
official 
does not have to disqualify hinself
or herself fron a governnental decision if:
(1) The decision only affects the
salary, per dien, or reirnbursenent for
expenses the official 
or his or her
spouse receives from a state or local
governnent agency. This subsection does
not apply to decisions to hire, fire,
promote, denote, or discipline an
official,s 
spouse, or to set a salary for
an official's 
spouse which is different
fron salaries paid to other enployees of
the spouse's agency in the sane job
classification or position;
Regulation ]-8702.1(c) (1) .
Therefore, a decision to increase the salaries of all
teachers in the same classification as LIs. Botwin would not create
a conflict of interest for her, even if it would increase her
income by SZSO. Ho$rever, a decision to increase or decrease onl-y
Ms. Botwj,n's salary by S250 or nore per year, as opposed to the
salaries of all the other teachers in her classifitition, 
hrouLd


. our Pile No. I-9o-155
Page 4
require her disqualification. 
Ms. Botwin would also be restricted
fr6rn participating in any disciplinary matters concerning herself'
(See Advice Letters to waqqoner, No. I-86-208. Rice, No. A-88-o53,
walker, No. A-89-595 and James, No. A-88-459, copies enclosed. )
You have also inquired about the nature of collective
bargaining units and whether they are considered a business
entity. 
iccording to Section 82005, a business entity is an
organization or enterprise operated for profit. 
Therefore, under
th6 Act, l1s. Botwin's certificated bargaining unit is not a
business entity for conf L ict-of- interest purposes. Accordingly,
Ms. Botwin may participate in collective bargaining decisions
concerning hei targaining unit as long as these decisions affect
herself aid atl employees in the same job classification in the
same manner.
The Comrnission has held that pension benefits received from
the county are al-so not considered income within the meaning of
Section eiolo(a). 
Pension benefits from a local governmental
agency ar" con"idered deferred salary and are therefore excluded
fion tne definition of income in Section 82030(b). (In re Moore
(L9'17) 3 FPPC ops. 33, copy enclosed' )
Thus, absent some other disqualifying financial interest as
set forth in Section 87103, Ms. Botwin may participate in board
decisions concerning the school district' 
However, as stated
above, our advice ii tinitea only to the PoliticaL Reform Act. 
As
y"" n""" informed ne, some of the.other issues are the subject of
lxtended Iitigation 
and the case is currently before the court of
appeals.
I trust this answers your questions. If you have any further
questions concerning this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5901.
s incere Iy ,
scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel

By: Ji,A!. stecher
Cchhsel, Legal Divis ion
SH : JL: KEN
Enc Iosures
